cases.name: empty_workspace
expected: runtime files are created immediately
runtime/insights.json is created
runtime/orchestration_contract.json is created
minimum gate is not satisfied
next_best_input has no more than 3 questions
final pack can render blocked pages without raw runtime leakage

cases.name: partial_offer_notes

Offer: AI CRM cleanup for small agencies
ICP: agency owners
Channel: cold outbound
expected: offer, ICP, and primary channel are filled
target KPI remains missing
funnel recommendations remain blocked

cases.name: no_proof_yet
cases.input:
Offer: weekly conversion audits
ICP: SaaS founders
Target KPI: trial activation
Channel: LinkedIn content
No proof yet
expected: minimum gate is satisfied
recommendations_ready remains false until research readiness reaches ready state
post-gate next_best_input has no more than 2 topic-specific clarify questions
qualification score is lower than with proof
no proof claims are fabricated
promise_proof_model marks the main promise as no_proof with an assumption fallback
variant_bundles keep no-proof copy or CTA rows blocked with assumption_ids and blocked_reason
final pack renders clean Markdown and HTML
recommendations cite evidence refs or explicit assumptions

cases.name: research_with_competitors
cases.input:
Offer: connect Stripe and see churn risks in 3 minutes
ICP: SaaS operators
Target KPI: first value reached
Channel: search
Proof: customer case showed 18% churn recovery
Competitor: RivalOne | domain: rivalone.com | pricing: $29/mo | CTA: Start free | onboarding: connect Stripe | source: https://rivalone.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: RivalTwo | domain: rivaltwo.com | pricing: $49/mo | CTA: Book demo | onboarding: import billing data | source: https://rivaltwo.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: RivalThree | domain: rivalthree.com | pricing: custom | CTA: View demo | onboarding: sample workspace | source: https://rivalthree.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
expected: source registry includes URL, publisher, retrieval date, type, confidence, and used_in
competitor map includes pricing, CTA, onboarding, and source
phase is ready when research readiness is at least 60 and there are no contradictions
insights include at least 3 actionable screen-level recommendations
runtime/insights.json includes competitor_synthesis with observed pricing, CTA, and onboarding patterns
runtime/insights.json includes promise_proof_model linking the promise, objection, proof requirement, and evidence status
runtime/insights.json includes 2-3 variant_bundles with hypothesis, proof requirement, measurement_event, claim_ids, and source_ids or assumption_ids
runtime/insights.json includes reviewer_approval and leaves normal non-risky source-backed cases not_required
runtime/orchestration_contract.json maps tasks to input_refs, output_refs, claim_ids, source_ids, assumption_ids, status, and blocked_reason
final competitor map renders synthesized patterns and source IDs without leaking raw JSON
final screen specs render variant bundles as a table without raw variant_bundles JSON
final pages do not leak raw orchestration contract JSON
recommendations visibly use competitor-derived differentiation rather than only listing competitors
final report separates evidence from recommendations
HTML provides visual decision aids beyond plain Markdown styling

cases.name: russian_workspace
cases.input:
Оффер: аудит онбординга для SaaS
ICP: основатели SaaS
Целевой KPI: активация триала
Канал: LinkedIn content
Нет доказательств
expected: runtime output_language is Russian
default English workspaces switch to Russian after Russian ingest
final index and status pages render Russian headings
final HTML contains inline styles and navigation
Russian output has no leftover English UI strings like Intake brief, Previous, Next, or Auto-collect

cases.name: p0_b2b_saas_fast_ttfv_contract
cases.input:
Offer: connect Stripe and CRM to show churn-risk accounts in 3 minutes
ICP: B2B SaaS founders, growth leads, and RevOps operators
Target KPI: First Value Reached / Trial Started
Channel: search
TTFV: 3
Proof: customer case showed reduced churn-risk response time
Competitor: RivalOne | domain: rivalone.com | positioning: churn analytics | pricing: $29/mo | CTA: Start free | onboarding: connect Stripe | source: https://rivalone.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: RivalTwo | domain: rivaltwo.com | positioning: revenue recovery | pricing: $49/mo | CTA: Book demo | onboarding: import billing data | source: https://rivaltwo.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: RivalThree | domain: rivalthree.com | positioning: retention workflows | pricing: custom | CTA: View demo | onboarding: sample workspace | source: https://rivalthree.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
expected: insights include evidence_claims with claim_id and source_ids
screen and experiment recommendations include claim_ids, source_ids or assumption_ids, owner_action, and measurement_event

experiment recommendations include event_id, guardrail_metrics, exposure_definition, event_instrumentation, SRM check, event_loss_threshold, expected_effect_range, stop/ship/iterate rules, and failure_mode
channel_synthesis primary pack is search and includes SearchIntentMatched or equivalent search event ids
runtime/insights.json includes niche_profile=saas with SaaS vocabulary, risks, proof patterns, funnel defaults, and event suggestions
final funnel blueprint renders a channel route table with primary channel, support loops, event IDs, and risks
final pages render the SaaS niche profile and profile event suggestions without raw niche_profile JSON
final experiment card renders quality gates and decision thresholds without raw JSON

export_launch.py writes action_plan, event_schema, content_brief, CRM handoff, funnel_diff, variant_bundles, and experiment_card JSON/CSV into exports/ with claim_ids, source_ids, assumption_ids, and blocked_reason
export_launch.py writes reviewer_approval JSON/CSV into exports/ and never into final/
export_launch.py writes orchestration_contract JSON/CSV into exports/ and includes it in manifest.json
variant_bundles use channel_synthesis, niche_profile, competitor_synthesis, and current_funnel_diff signals when available without inventing proof or metrics
promise_proof_model records the promise-proof status and blocks unsupported risky claims
promise_proof_model includes proof mechanics guidance by claim type, sales motion, and risk level, but treats it as guidance rather than evidence
final competitor map shows source-backed differentiation patterns from competitor_synthesis
first-value route is specific to billing/account risk rather than generic conversion advice
unsupported revenue recovery claims are blocked or marked as assumptions

cases.name: p0_current_funnel_diff_contract
cases.input:
Offer: connect Stripe and CRM to show churn-risk accounts in 3 minutes
ICP: B2B SaaS founders and RevOps operators
Target KPI: First Value Reached / Trial Started
Channel: search
Current funnel: Google ad -> generic landing page -> signup form -> manual sales call
TTFV: 3
Proof: customer case showed reduced churn-risk response time
Competitor: RivalOne | domain: rivalone.com | positioning: churn analytics | pricing: $29/mo | CTA: Start free | onboarding: connect Stripe | source: https://rivalone.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: RivalTwo | domain: rivaltwo.com | positioning: revenue recovery | pricing: $49/mo | CTA: Book demo | onboarding: import billing data | source: https://rivaltwo.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: RivalThree | domain: rivalthree.com | positioning: retention workflows | pricing: custom | CTA: View demo | onboarding: sample workspace | source: https://rivalthree.com/pricing | retrieved: 2026-05-15
expected: runtime/insights.json includes current_funnel_diff with raw_current_steps and row-level change_type
current_funnel_diff maps provided current steps to proposed screen/channel/niche steps without inventing current metrics, tools, copy, or conversion rates
current_funnel_diff rows include measurement_event, claim_ids, source_ids or assumption_ids, and blocked_reason
variant_bundles include current_step or control_reference from current_funnel_diff when current funnel context is available
final funnel blueprint renders a Current vs Proposed Changes table without raw JSON
exports/funnel_diff.json and .csv provide a brief machine-readable handoff outside final/

cases.name: p1_experiment_quality_gates_low_traffic
cases.input:
Offer: expert onboarding audit for B2B SaaS
ICP: SaaS founders
Target KPI: qualified activation calls
Channel: LinkedIn content
TTFV: 5
Proof: customer case https://case.example/onboarding retrieved 2026-05-15
Metric: traffic 30 sessions last month
Competitor: AuditOne | domain: auditone.example | positioning: onboarding audits | CTA: Get audit | onboarding: role questionnaire | proof: case study | source: https://auditone.example/audit | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: AuditTwo | domain: audittwo.example | positioning: activation consulting | CTA: Book review | onboarding: diagnostic form | proof: testimonials | source: https://audittwo.example/review | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: AuditThree | domain: auditthree.example | positioning: trial activation help | CTA: Start diagnostic | onboarding: benchmark quiz | proof: customer story | source: https://auditthree.example/diagnostic | retrieved: 2026-05-15
expected: experiment card includes hypothesis, target segment, primary metric, guardrail metrics, exposure definition, event instrumentation, SRM check, event loss threshold, expected effect range, stop/ship/iterate rules, and failure mode
measurement_event equals a concrete event_id rather than a raw KPI label
low-traffic case uses qualitative or sequential learning plan and does not claim statistical lift
renderer exposes SRM, event loss, expected effect, and stop/ship/iterate thresholds clearly

cases.name: p0_telegram_webinar_contract
cases.input:
Offer: education program for entrepreneurs using Meta Ads to Telegram bot to webinar to strategy call
ICP: Ukrainian entrepreneurs in Europe
Target KPI: qualified showed strategy calls
Channel: Meta Ads, Telegram bot, webinar, CRM follow-up
No proof yet
Competitor: CourseOne | domain: courseone.example | positioning: webinar-first education | CTA: Join webinar | onboarding: Telegram quiz | source: https://courseone.example/webinar | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: CourseTwo | domain: coursetwo.example | positioning: bot-led qualification | CTA: Start bot | onboarding: intent branch | source: https://coursetwo.example/bot | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: CourseThree | domain: coursethree.example | positioning: consultation funnel | CTA: Book call | onboarding: webinar replay | source: https://coursethree.example/call | retrieved: 2026-05-15
expected: bot, webinar, and CRM recommendations are represented as structured recommendations with measurement_event
channel_synthesis chooses Meta as the primary pack and represents Telegram, Webinar, and Email/CRM as support loops
tracking events include channel-specific IDs for ad, bot/webinar/CRM handoff logic
runtime/insights.json includes niche_profile=education with education vocabulary, risks, proof patterns, funnel defaults, and event suggestions
final pages render the education niche profile without raw niche_profile JSON
final report renders primary channel and support loops without raw channel_synthesis JSON
promise_proof_model marks no-proof promises as assumptions with blocked_reason
variant_bundles inherit no-proof assumption_ids and blocked_reason instead of marking copy or CTA variants ready
proof mechanics guidance recommends a proof format but does not treat the recommendation as evidence
income claims remain blocked because proof is missing
recommendations use assumption_ids when evidence is insufficient
machine-readable launch exports remain blocked and do not mark rows ready while phase is draft or research
phase remains draft/research unless evidence quality is sufficient

cases.name: p0_high_ticket_real_estate_contract
cases.input:
Offer: overseas real estate consultation and property shortlist
ICP: investors and lifestyle relocation buyers
Target KPI: qualified consultation booked and showed
Channel: paid social to qualification form to consultation
No proof yet
Competitor: EstateOne | domain: estateone.example | positioning: investment property advisory | CTA: Book consultation | onboarding: budget questionnaire | source: https://estateone.example/advisory | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: EstateTwo | domain: estatetwo.example | positioning: relocation property search | CTA: Get shortlist | onboarding: country fit quiz | source: https://estatetwo.example/relocation | retrieved: 2026-05-15
Competitor: EstateThree | domain: estatethree.example | positioning: premium buyer support | CTA: Talk to advisor | onboarding: legal readiness check | source: https://estatethree.example/buyers | retrieved: 2026-05-15
expected: risky legal, payment, or return claims are blocked or routed through assumptions
paid social or Meta path includes message prequalification and lead quality risk instead of a generic consultation CTA
runtime/insights.json includes niche_profile=real_estate with buyer vocabulary, trust/qualification risks, proof patterns, funnel defaults, and event suggestions
final pages render the real estate niche profile without raw niche_profile JSON
competitor_synthesis renders trust/qualification patterns without inventing legal or return proof
promise_proof_model flags risky legal/payment/return promises without source-backed proof
proof mechanics guidance does not convert risky_unverified, weak_proof, or no_proof into ready
source-backed high-risk promises still require explicit human reviewer approval before phase=ready
human reviewer approval does not unblock missing, weak, stale, or low-weight evidence
trust-path recommendations include structured support fields
generic consultation recommendations without claim_ids or support fail contract validation
ready phase is blocked without strong evidence despite a renderable draft report
